sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The cortex indexer scans a Python project and builds a knowledge graph stored in `.cortex/cortex.db`. It tracks every function, module, and document — along with their relationships, change history, and documentation staleness. The index powers all other cortex features: the viz dashboard, MCP tools, and the CLI query commands.

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
```bash
# Build the index (discovery-only — safe to run repeatedly)
cortex build .

# Check for stale documentation
cortex check .

# List all functions
cortex list . --type atomic_process

# Search for a symbol
cortex render . --level 1 --id "myproject::pm.database::get_engine"
```

The first `cortex build` creates `.cortex/cortex.db` and indexes everything. Subsequent builds only add newly-discovered nodes — existing staleness signals are preserved.

sections.heading: Core Functionality
sections.content:
- **Build** (`cortex build`): Scans .py files via AST and docs/**/*.json via the DocJSON scanner. Generates nodes, edges, tags, and FTS entries. Discovery-only by default; `--full` resets all baselines.
- **Check** (`cortex check`): Reports per-node staleness. Fast mtime check, then per-function hash comparison. Outputs summary + problem nodes. Use `--fail-on stale` in CI.
- **List** (`cortex list`): Enumerate nodes filtered by `--type` and/or `--tag`. Output is level-1 summaries.
- **Render** (`cortex render`): Display nodes at detail levels 0 (IDs only), 1 (summary), 2 (full detail), or steps (dFlow step markers).
- **Graph** (`cortex graph`): Traverse edges from a node. `--direction in` shows callers; `--direction out` shows dependencies.
- **Link** (`cortex link`): Manually add typed edges (e.g. `validates`, `documents`) between nodes.
- **Mark-clean** (`cortex mark-clean`): Record a human verification on a DESC_STALE node.
- **History** (`cortex history checkpoint`): Collapse old history rows. `cortex history agent-verified` lists AI-reviewed nodes for human pre-push review.
- **Test** (`cortex test`): Run pytest, parse results, map to cortex nodes, store in DB.
- **Export** (`cortex export`): Dump the full index to `.cortex/index.json`.
- **Search**: Full-text search via `cortex render --level 1` filtered output, or via the MCP/viz search endpoints.

sections.heading: Configuration & Options
sections.content:
| Command | Key Options | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cortex build` | `--full` | Full rebuild (resets baselines) vs discovery-only (default) |
| `cortex build` | `--id <prefix>` | Custom project ID prefix (default: directory name) |
| `cortex check` | `--fail-on <level>` | Exit 1 on: `none`, `stale`, `unverified`, `any` |
| `cortex check` | `--all` | Show all nodes including CLEAN (default: problems only) |
| `cortex check` | `--format json` | Machine-readable JSON output |
| `cortex render` | `--level <0\|1\|2\|steps>` | Detail level |
| `cortex graph` | `--direction <in\|out\|both>` | Edge traversal direction |
| `cortex graph` | `--depth N` | Number of hops |
| `cortex test` | `--node <id>` | Scope to tests validating this node |
| `cortex test` | `--path <prefix>` | Scope to tests covering nodes under this path |
| `cortex test` | `--no-save` | Run tests without writing results to DB |
| `cortex test` | `--runner <cmd>` | Override test runner (e.g. `poetry run pytest`) |
| `cortex history checkpoint` | `--keep N` | Rows to retain per node after prune |
| `cortex history checkpoint` | `--older-than N` | Only collapse entries older than N days |

sections.heading: Best Practices & Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### Best Practices
- Run `cortex build .` after pulling new code or before starting a coding session
- Use `cortex check . --fail-on stale` in CI to prevent merging stale docs
- Use `cortex history checkpoint . --no-prune` before releases to snapshot state
- Add `.cortex/` to `.gitignore` — the index is always rebuilt from source

### Common Issues
- **Problem:** `No index found at .cortex/cortex.db`
- **Solution:** Run `cortex build .` first.

- **Problem:** Everything shows as UNVERIFIED after first build
- **Solution:** This is expected — UNVERIFIED means no prior baseline exists. Run `cortex build --full .` to establish baselines.

- **Problem:** STRUCTURAL_DRIFT on a renamed function
- **Solution:** The old node is orphaned. Run `cortex build .` to discover the new function, then `cortex link` to reconnect edges.

- **Problem:** `cortex test` shows many unmapped tests
- **Solution:** Use `cortex link <test_node> --edge-type validates --to <prod_node> .` to connect tests to the code they cover.
